title: Message Queues
subtitle: Async decoupling between producers and consumers with guaranteed delivery

nodes.label: Producers
nodes.description: Services that publish messages: API servers, microservices, IoT devices

nodes.label: Message Queue
nodes.description: Durable buffer that stores messages until consumers process them
items: Decouples producer speed from consumer speed
Guarantees at-least-once delivery
Persists messages across consumer restarts
Acts as traffic smoother under burst load

nodes.label: Queue Patterns
items: Point-to-Point: 1 producer → 1 consumer (SQS)
Pub/Sub: 1 producer → N consumers (SNS, Kafka)
FIFO: strict ordered delivery (SQS FIFO)
Dead Letter Queue: failed message storage

nodes.label: Consumers
nodes.description: Services that process messages at their own pace
items: Pull model: consumer polls the queue
Push model: queue triggers consumer (Lambda)
Scale consumers independently from producers
Visibility timeout: message hidden during processing

nodes.label: Dead Letter Queue
nodes.description: Captures messages that fail processing after max retries
items: After N failures → message moved to DLQ
Alert + investigate DLQ messages
Replay from DLQ after bug fix
Essential for production reliability

nodes.label: Ordering & Idempotency
nodes.description: How to handle message ordering and duplicate delivery
items: At-most-once: fire-and-forget (may lose)
At-least-once: may deliver duplicates → idempotent consumers
Exactly-once: Kafka transactions, SQS FIFO + dedup ID
Idempotency key: same msg = same result

nodes.label: AWS Services
nodes.description: Managed queue and streaming services on AWS
items: SQS Standard: high throughput, best-effort order
SQS FIFO: 3000 msg/s, strict ordering
SNS: fan-out pub/sub to multiple SQS
Kafka (MSK): streaming, 1M+ msg/s, replay
